```

#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"

	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Philips 170S"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Monitor		"Philips 170S"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1280x960" "1152x864" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

```

Thanks all

### Post by DrMega on 2007-12-20
I'm not sure what you are asking. Are you saying you HAVE configured TV out or that you WANT to?

If you want to configure TV out, you need to decide on a number of things:

1. Do you want the TV to be a clone of the monitor, or will it form part of a large desktop?

2. Once set up, do you intend to still have a monitor connected, or will the TV be the only "monitor" in your final setup?

I've had to do this a number of times for my media centre (every time I do a rebuild and forget to backup my xorg.conf:( ) so I'm sure I can help you once I know what you have done/are trying to do.

In my experience there is a little quirk with Gutsy and TV out (maybe just ATI cards, I don't know), so if you do have trouble, let me know and I'll post up my solution.

---

### Post by ema85b on 2007-12-20
Sorry for my english.

I want to configure TV out

1. I want clone monitor

### Post by DrMega on 2007-12-20
I'm not in front of my Ubuntu box at the moment so I can't remember the exact syntax of the command you need. I can get you it later, but in the meantime, if you feel confident enough to run the config command and ref to its help, this is the general procedure:

1. Open a terminal

2. Backup your xorg.conf

```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /ect/X11/xorg.conf.backup
```

3. Run the following command (I can't remember if the hyphen should be there or not):

```
ati-config
```

4. That will output its help, and in there there is an example for doing the initial setup for 1 monitor+1 TV

5. Run that ati-config with the params from the help example:

```
sudo ati-config --forcemonitors=crt0,tv (or whatever it is)
```

6. Restart X (CTRL+ALT+Backspace)

Sorry I can't give you the exact command right now, I'll get it later for you.

As you intend to have a monitor connect as well, you won't be affected by the same issue I had (video settings reset during boot, and certain video apps crash - both incredibly easy to fix if you do hit them).

---

### Post by ema85b on 2007-12-20
Thanks for all DrMega i have try but no work. The Radeon 9200 is not supported with new official drive. For resolve this problem today i have buy the new card Nvidia FX5500. I have install the driver with Envy [http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html) . 
Now work great tv-out ;)

---

I thought everything from the 8000 series upwards was supported. I have a 9550 which works fine. It figures though, ATI drivers are troublesome whether under Windows or Linux. nVidia drivers tend to be much better.

Anyway, I'm glad you've got it all working.

### Post by Hellmark on 2008-02-20
The latest drivers no longer have support for the R200 chipset, which is the 8500-9200. 9500 and above is the R300 chipset.

---
